dominant_palette: matte black, antique gold, cream-stone white, oxidised silver
secondary_palette: amethyst purple, deep ruby red, warm sand
composition_vocabulary: Jewellery pieces are shot tight and centred against near-black grounds; stone fragments are photographed at slight angles to show raw, uneven edges. No lifestyle staging — every tile reads as a studied document of an object rather than a scene.
lighting_quality: Chiaroscuro studio light throughout — hard directional sources that carve deep shadow around metal and stone, giving every object a relic-like weight.
material_textures: rough broken stone, polished cast gold, oxidised silver, cabochon gemstone, carved relief surface, aged metal patina
aesthetic_register: Devotional heirloom — the sample sits squarely in the territory of sacred objects and relics rather than contemporary fine jewellery or streetwear; the tone is closer to a church treasury than a showcase.
notably_absent: There are no models, no wrist or hand shots contextualising scale, no draped fabric or surface styling — the human body and any sense of the piece being worn are completely absent.
